## AEC — Agent Environment Config

AEC (`agents-environment-config`) is a CLI that manages five item types
across multiple AI agents: agents, skills, rules, packages, and plugins.

You may use AEC to manage these items as part of your workflow, within the
boundaries listed below. Run `aec --help` for full command reference.

### Operations you may run without asking

- `aec discover` — read-only (always auto)
- `aec discover-repos` — read-only (always auto)
- `aec doctor` — read-only (always auto)
- `aec info` — read-only (always auto)
- `aec list` — read-only (always auto)
- `aec outdated` — read-only (always auto)
- `aec search` — read-only (always auto)
- `aec validate` — read-only (always auto)
- `aec version` — read-only (always auto)
- `aec install skill <name>` — install a skill (skills: additive=auto)
- `aec install rule <name>` — install a rule (rules: additive=auto)

### Operations that require explicit user permission

- `aec install agent <name>` (agents: additive=ask)
- `aec install package <name>` (packages: additive=ask)
- `aec install plugin <name>` (plugins: additive=ask)
- `aec configure-agent` — destructive (always ask)
- `aec prune` — destructive (always ask)
- `aec setup` — destructive (always ask)
- `aec uninstall` — destructive (always ask)
- `aec untrack` — destructive (always ask)
- `aec update` — destructive (always ask)
- `aec upgrade` — destructive (always ask)

### How to interpret this block

This block is managed by AEC. Do not hand-edit between the start/end markers;
use `aec configure-agent` to change scope or profile. The `content-hash` in
the start marker is recomputed on every render; if you must hand-edit, run
`aec configure-agent --check` to see the drift report and `--refresh` to
re-render.

<!-- aec-blurb:end -->
